ab: ne hucma, mo’u uhto huehungu ho aiti ’o ak’i.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: micu noana’o ho o’ana mimza ahtu yupa bobaito, os’o na’na taecingia.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven't seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: aiti to ak’i a’o, osnia pa’ni to vahatʉ a’o, pema to ’ocea.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: na’no cohʉmʉ ’o isi emʉ’a to ak’i ci vahatʉ, aʉmtʉ na’no mafe.
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: os’o yaeza ʉmnʉa ’o ’ocea to ak’i, maezo mafe ho isi ima.
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
en: I also like grandpa's tea, it's tasted good too.

ab: cu cohiona, mimza iepoepohʉ ’o ba’i ho apapeipei’i tote ana no cohzona.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: na’no toemamave ’o bai homo apapeipei’i to ’oanʉ no cohiona, mo yaa yoskʉa, yaa teo’ua, yaa pusiahʉa, maezo yaa kokeut’ee.
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: oepʉngʉ ocohioni, mimza uhto chachae ho toa chae
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: man’i ’o isi yaa to ba’i ci f’ue, ucei ho t’ocnga faeni a’o, pahafneni a’o maine’e.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: mimza yupa tooyokeo, tamzala vohvaho no ta’esi ho aiti hin’i.
ch: 我跟他們道別，說下次再去看他們。
en: I said goodbye to them and said I'd see them next time.
